content.body: Noam Rosenthal, annevk: it looks like neither Chrome nor Safari does [this](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#navigate-fragid:~:text=For%20other%20fragment%20navigations%2C%20including%20user%2Dinitiated%20ones%2C%20the%20navigation%20API%20state%20is%20carried%20over%20from%20the%20previous%20entry). is there a reason for that?
content.formatted_body: Noam Rosenthal, annevk: it looks like neither Chrome nor Safari does this. is there a reason for that?

content.body:
and if we don't want carry over to be the default for non-Navigation API navigations, should we add a 
```
enum NavigationStateLifetimeBehavior {
  "none",
  "carryOver"
}
```
value to the options of the navigation api methods (including updateCurrentEntry)?
content.formatted_body:
and if we don't want carry over to be the default for non-Navigation API navigations, should we add a

enum NavigationStateLifetimeBehavior {
  "none",
  "carryOver"
}

value to the options of the navigation api methods (including updateCurrentEntry)?

content.body: I'm not sure. Is there a test Chrome and Safari fail?
but we've been discussing a bit around what is actually preferable
I suspect you and Jake Archibald are probably a better judge of that than I am.
content.formatted_body: I suspect you and Jake Archibald are probably a better judge of that than I am.

content.body:
So for Ladybird, I have implemented support for downloading `application/octet-stream` resources, and for other content types we don’t support. See <https://github.com/LadybirdBrowser/ladybird/issues/10238>. And now I am at the point of essentially just trying to cleanly retrofit/align a “hook” (as it were) for my already-implemented (conforming) handle-as-a-download behavior with the actual spec requirements.

So I am looking at step 3 and step 4 of https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/browsing-the-web.html#loading-a-document. 

And I am not implementing step 3 at all — because we’re not going to do _“displaying some sort of inline content, e.g., a native rendering of the content or an error message”_.

So OK then I move on to step 4. We’re not going to do _“Hand-off to external software”_. What we‘re doing is just _“handle as a download”._
content.formatted_body:
So for Ladybird, I have implemented support for downloading application/octet-stream resources, and for other content types we don’t support. See https://github.com/LadybirdBrowser/ladybird/issues/10238. And now I am at the point of essentially just trying to cleanly retrofit/align a “hook” (as it were) for my already-implemented (conforming) handle-as-a-download behavior with the actual spec requirements.

So I am looking at step 3 and step 4 of https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/browsing-the-web.html#loading-a-document.

And I am not implementing step 3 at all — because we’re not going to do “displaying some sort of inline content, e.g., a native rendering of the content or an error message”.

So OK then I move on to step 4. We’re not going to do “Hand-off to external software”. What we‘re doing is just “handle as a download”.
